metadata_mapping.scene_title: h1.entry-title text on the post page; also available in the HTML <title>
metadata_mapping.date: absolute post date in the entry meta/byline block, formatted like 'May 9, 2026'
metadata_mapping.description: main WordPress post content inside the entry-content/article body
metadata_mapping.cover_image: prefer og:image meta; fallback to first post image or embedded video thumbnail if present
metadata_mapping.performers: infer from tag links on the post page; the site does not have dedicated performer pages
metadata_mapping.tags: tag links on the post page, usually under /index.php/tag/{slug}/
metadata_mapping.studio: Gay Fappy (constant site/studio value, since posts are aggregator-style blog entries)

date: text near author/byline containing a full calendar date
description: strip boilerplate like 'More' links and unrelated navigation text
performers: tag inference should be conservative because tags mix performers and topical labels
search_results: a[href*='/index.php/'] with title text from result cards

search_strategy.notes: Use WordPress site search. Parse returned post cards/articles, then filter for likely scene posts. Prefer URLs matching /index.php/{id}/{slug}/ and optionally bias toward posts in the Videos category when category labels are available.

fragment_matching_strategy.notes: Use the filename fragment or title fragment as the search query against the WordPress search endpoint. Rank search results with normalized string similarity before scraping the top candidate.

performer_page_fields.notes: No dedicated performer pages were identified. Tags appear to be the only performer-like entity pages, so performerByURL is not recommended for the first implementation.

anti_bot_or_access_notes: No Cloudflare or captcha challenge was observed during inspection
Use a browser-like User-Agent header on requests
WordPress pages appear server-rendered and should parse cleanly with requests + BeautifulSoup
known_limitations: The site mixes photos, gifs, and videos; search results may include non-video posts unless filtered carefully
Performer names are not strongly normalized and are mixed with topical tags
No dedicated performer pages were identified, so performer scraping is weak or not applicable
Cover image may require fallback logic when og:image is missing or low quality
Aggregator-style posts may not expose a true source studio beyond the site name
